title: KEDA-Based GPU Swap Controller for Embed/Inference Coexistence

A.D1_notes: Core architectural misunderstanding: describes a custom gpu-swap-controller with pre/post lifecycle Jobs and explicit kubectl scale commands; GT uses stock K8s PriorityClass preemption with no custom controller. Jira is VW-224 (wrong, GT: VW-233). Kafka topic 'vault.embedding.requests' (wrong, GT: vault.connector-docs). lagThreshold effectively >0 (GT: 50). Cron at 02:00 UTC (GT: 03:00 UTC). Invents ResourceQuota safety net and pushgateway metrics not in GT. Fabricates ADR-012 circuit breaker reference. Uses VW-288 correctly as pipeline context but wrong Jira for connector (GT: VW-232). Hardware constraint and VRAM figures are correct. Alternatives are technically sound reasoning even if details differ.

missing_critical_references: HLD-03, HLD-05, HLD-06, HLD-07 (specific HLD modules)

A.D2_notes: Cites some real VW tickets (VW-288, VW-152, VW-180) and mentions ADR-018, but primary Jira is wrong (VW-224 vs VW-233), fabricates ADR-012, and misses most GT-specific references including VW-237, VW-232, VW-188, all four HLD modules, the related ADR pair (ADR-034/036), and implementation commits. 4 of 12 citable claims properly referenced.
A.D3_notes: All required top-level sections present (Context, Decision, Consequences pos+neg, Alternatives x4, Status, Related). Missing: dedicated Implementation References section (GT has one with 6 file paths), operational notes (manual override, model alignment), retry coupling section, NetworkPolicy discussion. Alternatives cover 4 of GT's 5 (missing: inline embedding inside vLLM). Swap budget table and Requirements sub-section are useful additions.
A.D4_notes: 2138 words vs GT's 1456 (47% longer). The custom gpu-swap-controller architecture with pre/post Jobs, ResourceQuota, pushgateway metrics, swap budget table, and ASCII architecture diagram add significant volume for a mechanism that GT implements more simply via PriorityClass preemption. Requirements sub-section and detailed Prefect alternative are reasonable but verbose.
A.D5_notes: Frontmatter has status/date/type/jira/tags but missing vault-path (GT has it). No wikilinks (GT uses [[HLD-03-AI-Layer]] etc.). Does not note retroactive ratification. Has comparison tables. Has pos/neg split. Missing related ADRs in frontmatter. No implementation commit references.

B.D1_notes: Technically accurate throughout. Correctly identifies VW-233 as Jira, VW-98 as predecessor, VW-203 as MacBook decommission trigger. PriorityClass values (1000/100) match GT. Kafka topic 'vault.file-events' (differs from GT's 'vault.connector-docs' but plausible); consumer group 'tei-embedding-consumer' vs GT's 'connector-indexer'. lagThreshold=1 vs GT's 50 -- minor config discrepancy. Cron 03:00-05:00 UTC matches GT. Correctly describes PriorityClass preemption with no custom controller. YAML snippets structurally correct. FluxCD SSA-ignore annotation is technically correct. Retry coupling matches GT exactly. Historical lineage (VW-98 -> VW-203 -> VW-233) is accurate.

specific_references_named: HLD-03 AI Layer (correct with vault path)
missing_critical_references: HLD-05, HLD-06, HLD-07 (3 of 4 GT HLD modules)

B.D2_notes: Strong citation density. Names VW-233, VW-98, VW-203, VW-107, VW-288, VW-96, VW-39 correctly. HLD-03 and HLD-02 with vault paths. Provides vault file paths for reports/plans. Missing GT's VW-237/VW-232, ADR-034/036, HLD-05/06/07, and implementation commits. Good historical lineage documentation. 9 of 12 citable claims referenced.
B.D3_notes: All required top-level sections. 3 alternatives (vLLM sleep mode, smaller model, second GPU) vs GT's 5 -- missing pure cron scheduling, inline embedding, and 'Run TEI on CPU' as distinct alternative. The vLLM sleep mode alternative is a strong addition with detailed technical analysis. Missing: Implementation References section, operational notes, NetworkPolicy discussion. Retry coupling present.
B.D4_notes: 2185 words vs GT's 1456 (50% longer). Historical lineage section adds ~300 words. vLLM sleep mode alternative is detailed (~200 words). YAML snippets add ~150 words. Several points stated twice (e.g., 'no custom controller'). Good content density but could be tightened 30%.
B.D5_notes: Frontmatter includes vault-path (matches GT). Related field in frontmatter. Tags relevant. Comparison table (VRAM). pos/neg split. No Obsidian wikilinks (GT uses [[HLD-03-AI-Layer]] etc.). Does not note retroactive ratification. Has Supersedes field (valid ADR pattern). YAML blocks demonstrate implementation.

C.D1_notes: Highly accurate. All three GT Jiras correct (VW-233, VW-237, VW-232) with URLs. Kafka config matches GT exactly: consumer-group 'connector-indexer', topic 'vault.connector-docs', lagThreshold=50. Cron 03:00-05:00 UTC, pollingInterval=30s, cooldownPeriod=300s, fallback.replicas=0 all match GT. PriorityClass preemption correctly described. TEI ~30s, vLLM ~60s match. Retry coupling exact match. VW-188 go-live date (2026-04-26) correct. NetworkPolicy commit 19d74f0 correct. Alert names differ slightly (KEDA-prefixed vs GT's GPU-prefixed) but both plausible. Model alignment note matches GT. Egress-identity-coupling lesson accurate.

specific_references_named: Implementation commits bb94571, a70b8f1, ea8161a, 19d74f0, 843bd7f (all match GT)

C.D2_notes: Excellent citation coverage. All three GT Jiras with URLs. Both related ADRs (ADR-034, ADR-036). All four HLD modules. All five implementation commits. CPU benchmark reference. NetworkPolicy commit and file. Additional references beyond GT (VW-203, VW-107, GPU-Swap-Topology doc, Ollama config). 11 of 12 citable claims referenced.
C.D3_notes: All sections present with high fidelity. 5 alternatives matching GT exactly (HAMi, Pure cron, TEI on CPU, Second GPU, Inline embedding). Implementation References with 7 file paths. Operational notes covering manual override and model alignment. Retry coupling. NetworkPolicy surface with egress-identity-coupling lesson. FluxCD coexistence section beyond GT. Retroactive ratification closing paragraph matches GT intent.
C.D4_notes: 2331 words vs GT's 1456 (60% longer). Most verbose condition. HAMi rejection duplicated in Context and Alternatives. Historical failure modes elaborated (median detection time, round-trip timing). FluxCD coexistence section (~80 words). NetworkPolicy surface section (~100 words). Some repetition. Content uniformly high-quality but could be 30% shorter.
C.D5_notes: Strong pattern adoption. Frontmatter has vault-path, status, date, type, jira, tags matching GT. Related ADRs in header with descriptions. Retroactive ratification in Status line and closing paragraph. Components table matches GT layout. pos/neg split. Implementation References section. One gap: no Obsidian wikilinks (GT uses [[HLD-03-AI-Layer]] etc., condition C uses plain-text bold references).

D.D1_notes: Mixed accuracy. Correctly identifies RTX 4080 16GB, ~15.6 GB vLLM footprint, ~8 GB TEI requirement. However: (1) Jira is VW-304 (wrong, GT: VW-233). (2) vLLM model identified as 'Qwen3.5-9B-AWQ' -- GT says DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B-AWQ; the model name is fabricated. (3) Kafka topics named 'vault-delta' and 'code-change' (wrong, GT: vault.connector-docs). (4) No PriorityClass mechanism described -- instead proposes a custom gpu-swap-controller architecture with pre-scale hooks, explicit kubectl scale commands, paused-replicas annotation, and pre/post lifecycle Jobs. This is the same core architectural misunderstanding as condition A: GT uses stock K8s PriorityClass preemption, not a custom controller. (5) lagThreshold implied as >0 (GT: 50). (6) Cron at midnight-ish rather than 03:00-05:00 UTC. (7) Cites 'typed memory' facts (fact 2131, 2183, 2215 etc.) -- these appear to be references to an AI memory system; their accuracy cannot be verified against GT which uses Jira+vault references instead. (8) vLLM prefix caching warm-up budget is not in GT. (9) TEI throughput claimed at '50-100 documents/second on GPU' (plausible but not in GT). Alternatives are technically sound reasoning.

specific_references_named: VW-304 (wrong Jira -- should be VW-233; VW-304 is described as 'SGLang vs. vLLM trial')
ADR-016 (correct reference but not in GT related ADRs)
missing_critical_references: HLD-03, HLD-05, HLD-06, HLD-07 (specific HLD modules)

D.D2_notes: Weakest citation performance. Primary Jira wrong (VW-304 vs VW-233). Cites VW-107, VW-203 correctly. References typed memory facts extensively (11 facts cited) but these are internal AI memory references, not the Jira/vault/git references the GT uses. No HLD module references. No related ADRs. No implementation commits. No NetworkPolicy references. No file paths to GitOps manifests. The typed memory citations are a fundamentally different citation paradigm from GT's Jira+vault+git approach. 3 of 12 citable GT-pattern claims referenced.
D.D3_notes: Has core sections (Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives, Related). 3 alternatives (second GPU, CPU-only embedding, smaller model) vs GT's 5 -- missing HAMi as explicit alternative, pure cron scheduling, and inline embedding inside vLLM. HAMi is discussed in context but not structured as a formal alternative. Missing: Implementation References section, operational notes (manual override, model alignment is load-bearing), retry coupling section, NetworkPolicy discussion. The 'typed memory' evidence approach is comprehensive within its own paradigm but does not match GT's structure. GitOps manifest paths absent. Neutral consequences section is an addition (GT has only positive and negative).
D.D4_notes: Approximately 2100 words vs GT's 1456 (44% longer). The typed memory citations add ~200 words of fact-number references. The custom gpu-swap-controller architecture with pre-scale hooks, paused-replicas annotation, and watchdog CronJob adds volume for a mechanism that GT implements more simply. The 'Why Manual Swap Is Untenable' section with specific toil estimates (15-50 minutes/day) is well-argued but verbose. vLLM prefix caching warm-up budget section adds content not in GT. CPU-only alternative discusses throughput figures at length.
D.D5_notes: Frontmatter has status/date/type/jira/tags but missing vault-path (GT has it). No wikilinks. Does not note retroactive ratification. Has comparison table (VRAM requirements). pos/neg/neutral split (GT only has pos/neg). No related ADRs in frontmatter or header. No implementation commit references. Relies on typed memory facts as citation paradigm rather than the Jira+vault+git paradigm GT uses. GitOps manifest locations absent from body.

summary.key_differentiator: Condition C matches GT on nearly all technical details (Jira numbers, Kafka config, commit SHAs, HLD references), correctly uses PriorityClass preemption as the swap mechanism, and cites the most references (11/12). Condition B is technically sound with the correct swap mechanism but misses VW-237/VW-232 and the related ADR pair. Conditions A and D both introduce fabricated custom controller architectures diverging from the deployed PriorityClass design; D additionally uses the wrong model name and relies on typed-memory facts rather than GT's Jira+vault citation paradigm. D edges below A due to weaker citation and pattern adoption despite similar technical issues.
